static_intro: litestar loaded. Prefer first-party Litestar patterns. Skills are namespaced as `litestar:<name>`. Available: litestar, litestar-routing, litestar-dto-openapi, litestar-auth-guards, litestar-di, litestar-data-services, litestar-settings, litestar-exceptions, litestar-middleware, litestar-plugins, litestar-realtime, litestar-ai-serving, sqlspec, advanced-alchemy, msgspec, litestar-granian, litestar-saq, litestar-vite, litestar-htmx, litestar-inertia, litestar-mcp, litestar-email, litestar-testing, pytest-databases, polyfactory, litestar-build, litestar-deployment, litestar-styleguide.

matchers.reminder: Project uses Litestar. Use `litestar:litestar` as a routing hub, then prefer the focused skill matching the work: routing, DTO/OpenAPI, auth/guards, DI, data/services, settings, exceptions, middleware, plugins, realtime, AI serving, testing, build, or deployment.
Controller, Router, or route decorator signals found. Use `litestar:litestar-routing` for handler signatures, route grouping, path parameters, response returns, and domain module layout.
DTO or OpenAPI signals found. Use `litestar:litestar-dto-openapi` for DTO selection, schema generation, request body encoding, OpenAPI config, and msgspec-vs-Pydantic fit.
Guard or auth-context signals found. Use `litestar:litestar-auth-guards` for authentication middleware boundaries, guards, permission failures, and keeping auth checks out of handlers.
Dependency injection signals found. Use `litestar:litestar-di` for Provide, layered dependency maps, request-scoped providers, and Dishka integration.
Data service or pagination signals found. Use `litestar:litestar-data-services` for repositories, service injection, filtering, pagination, and matching sqlspec or Advanced Alchemy.
Settings or environment loading signals found. Use `litestar:litestar-settings` for dataclass or pydantic-settings config, cached factories, env loading, and app-state wiring.
Exception handling signals found. Use `litestar:litestar-exceptions` for domain exception hierarchies, HTTP translation, handler registration, and RFC 7807-style responses.
Middleware signals found. Use `litestar:litestar-middleware` for middleware ordering, built-in config middleware, custom ASGI middleware, and request lifecycle concerns.
Plugin signals found. Use `litestar:litestar-plugins` for first-party and custom plugin protocols, init hooks, CLI registration, serialization plugins, and startup wiring.
WebSocket, SSE, or Channels signals found. Use `litestar:litestar-realtime` for socket handlers, ChannelsPlugin backends, ServerSentEvent streaming, and fan-out boundaries.
AI-serving signals found. Use `litestar:litestar-ai-serving` for serving Google ADK agents from Litestar with sqlspec-backed sessions and clear web/API boundaries.
Project imports sqlspec. Use the `litestar:sqlspec` skill for raw-SQL with adapter configurations, query mappings, and per-framework integration wiring (litestar/fastapi/flask/starlette extensions).

Project uses Advanced Alchemy / SQLAlchemy ORM. Use the `litestar:advanced-alchemy` skill for declarative models, services, repositories, and Alembic migrations — including the framework extension shipped for litestar/fastapi/flask/sanic/starlette.
Project uses msgspec. Use the `litestar:msgspec` skill for `Struct` definitions, `Meta` constraints, tagged unions, enc_hook/dec_hook patterns, and JSON/MessagePack serialization.
Project uses litestar-granian. Use the `litestar:litestar-granian` skill for the GranianPlugin (zero-config Rust-based ASGI replacement for `litestar run`).
Project uses litestar-saq. Use the `litestar:litestar-saq` skill for SAQPlugin / SAQConfig / QueueConfig: background tasks, cron jobs, and the `litestar workers run` CLI.

Project uses litestar-vite. Use the `litestar:litestar-vite` skill for VitePlugin / ViteConfig coordination between the Litestar app and a Vite frontend — PathConfig, RuntimeConfig, TypeGenConfig, and the `litestar assets` CLI.
Project uses litestar's HTMX integration. Use the `litestar:litestar-htmx` skill for HTMXPlugin, HTMXRequest, HTMXTemplate, TriggerEvent, PushUrl, and partial-template patterns.

Project uses Inertia.js with a Litestar backend. Use the `litestar:litestar-inertia` skill for InertiaConfig, component= routes, shared props, lazy props, and the React/Vue/Svelte client integration. Detection complements the `litestar-vite` signal — Inertia depends on Vite.
Project uses litestar-mcp. Use the `litestar:litestar-mcp` skill for LitestarMCP / MCPConfig and exposing route handlers as MCP tools/resources via JSON-RPC 2.0.
Project uses litestar-email. Use the `litestar:litestar-email` skill for EmailPlugin / EmailConfig / EmailService and the SMTP/Resend/SendGrid/Mailgun/InMemory backends.
Test code present. Use the `litestar:litestar-testing` skill for TestClient vs AsyncTestClient, `@pytest.mark.anyio`, app + lifespan fixtures, and Litestar-specific test patterns.
Project uses pytest-databases. Use the `litestar:pytest-databases` skill for container-based PostgreSQL/MySQL/SQLite/Oracle fixtures and Docker test integration.

Project uses polyfactory. Use the `litestar:polyfactory` skill for typed mock-data factories: pick the right base (`ModelFactory` Pydantic / `DataclassFactory` / `MsgspecFactory` / `AttrsFactory` / `SQLAlchemyFactory`), customize fields with `Use(...)` / `Fixture(...)`, register fixtures via `@register_fixture`, and parametrize over `coverage()` for discriminated unions. Pairs with `litestar:litestar-testing` for the request side.

Project ships a PyApp / bundler build. Use the `litestar:litestar-build` skill for `uv build` + `hatch build --target binary`, PyApp onefile binaries (configured via `PYAPP_*` env vars at build time + `[tool.hatch.build.targets.binary]` in pyproject), Hatchling `force-include` for bundled frontend assets, and GitHub Actions matrix workflows.

Project has deployment configs. Use the `litestar:litestar-deployment` skill for Granian-first containerization, systemd units, Kubernetes manifests (kustomize/helm), Terraform IaC, and platform-specific deploy patterns (Railway / Cloud Run / GKE / AlloyDB / Docker / Podman).
